articles.headline: NASA stops work on SLS Mobile Launcher 2
articles.blurb: NASA has stopped work on a second mobile launch platform intended for an upgraded version of the Space Launch System the agency no longer plans to develop.
articles.takeaway: NASA's termination of ML2 signals a strategic shift away from iterative SLS upgrades, potentially redirecting resources toward more ambitious deep-space architectures or commercial launch partnerships.

articles.headline: After impressive Elevated reveal, United surprises with basic tier

articles.takeaway: United's simultaneous launch of premium and basic service tiers signals a deliberate bifurcation strategy to capture both high-yield and price-sensitive segments amid competitive capacity pressures.

articles.headline: 5 Fastest Fighter Jets In Service Today
articles.blurb: The world's highest performance warbirds.
articles.takeaway: Modern fighter jets now exceed Mach 3+ speeds, but operational constraints and fuel consumption limit actual combat usage to subsonic-to-transonic regimes.

articles.headline: The Evolution of Aviation SMS
articles.blurb: From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.
articles.takeaway: Modern aviation SMS shifts accountability from post-accident investigations to continuous risk identification, reducing incidents before they occur rather than learning from them.

articles.headline: Shoes Off Again: TSA Under Pressure To Reverse "Dangerous" Shoes-On Policy
articles.blurb: This policy has been the subject of much discussion among analysts.
articles.takeaway: TSA's shoes-on screening policy, designed to accelerate checkpoint throughput, faces mounting pushback from security experts citing elevated risk tolerance as the tradeoff for operational efficiency.

articles.headline: Which US Airline Benefits Most From Buying JetBlue In 2026?
articles.blurb: The three buyers that JetBlue is targeting, with the benefits and drawbacks of each.
articles.takeaway: JetBlue's Northeast Alliance dominance and Caribbean route network could reshape competitive positioning for whichever acquirer absorbs its 100+ aircraft and high-yield leisure operations.

articles.headline: US Air Force’s MC-130Js Destroyed In Iran Cost Over $100 Million Each, Special Ops Says
articles.blurb: USAF destroys $100M MC-130Js in Iran rescue mission, revealing the extreme risks, advanced tactics, and rising aircraft losses in contested airspace.
articles.takeaway: The destruction of these $100M+ aircraft underscores how contested airspace increasingly forces military operators to choose between mission success and asset preservation, raising questions about future special operations viability in peer-conflict scenarios.

articles.headline: How to Assess Safety Risk
articles.blurb: A practical walkthrough of safety risk assessment under 14 CFR Part 5.

articles.headline: The Striking Differences Between The Boeing 737 MAX & Airbus A320neo Families

articles.takeaway: The 737 MAX's larger fuselage capacity directly challenges the A320neo's market dominance by enabling higher per-flight revenue potential on identical routes.

articles.headline: Crossing the ‘Line of Death’ at 2,125 MPH: SR-71 Battle Damage Assessment after ‘El Dorado Canyon’

articles.takeaway: The SR-71's post-strike reconnaissance role demonstrated how hypersonic platforms provided irreplaceable real-time battle damage assessment capabilities that justified extreme operational risk.

articles.headline: Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization
articles.blurb: Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.
articles.takeaway: Operational optimization across all aviation layers offers a cost-effective pathway to accommodate growth without proportional emissions increases, making sustainability economically aligned with business expansion.
keywords: aviation; optimization; operations research; sustainability; environmental impacts

articles.headline: NASA finalizes science plans for Artemis 2 lunar flyby
